definition:
πύθων, -ωνος, ὁ 
   1. in cl., Python, a serpent slain by Apollo, who is hence surnamed the Pythian. 
   2. In Plut. (ii, 414 E), a name given to ventriloquist soothsayers (ἐγγαστρίμυθοι; cf. Lev.19:31 20:6, 27, 1Ki.28:7), and perhaps in this sense πνεῦμα πύθωνα, a python-spirit: Act.16:16.†
 (AS)
